org.key_project.util.collection

Class DefaultImmutableSet<T>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.key_project.util.collection.DefaultImmutableSet<T>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Iterable<T>, ImmutableSet<T>

    public class DefaultImmutableSet<T>
extends java.lang.Object
implements ImmutableSet<T>
    implementation of a persistent set using the SLListOf implementation with all its implications (means e.g. O(n) for adding an element and so on.
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static int UNION_OPTIMIZATION_SIZE
      Constant defining the set size at which an optimized union operation will be executed.

        public ImmutableSet<T> add(T element)
        adds an element
        Specified by:
        add in interface ImmutableSet<T>
        Parameters:
        element - of type that has to be added to this set
        Returns:
        a set containing all elements of this one and the specified element.

      • addUnique

        public ImmutableSet<T> addUnique(T element)
                          throws NotUniqueException
        adds an element, barfs if the element is already present
        Specified by:
        addUnique in interface ImmutableSet<T>
        Parameters:
        element - of type that has to be added to this set
        Throws:
        NotUniqueException - if the element is already present

      • fromImmutableList

        public static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromImmutableList(ImmutableList<T> list)
        Create an immutable set from an immutable list.
        Parameters:
        list - a non-null immutable list
        Returns:
        a fresh immutable set with the same iteration order.

      • fromSet

        public static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromSet(@Nullable
                                          java.util.Set<T> set)
        Create an immutable set from a mutable set
        Specified by:
        fromSet in interface ImmutableSet<T>
        Parameters:
        set - a non-null mutable set
        Returns:
        a fresh immutable set with all the elements in set
